title: Thai Empanada Spring Rolls
description: A fusion twist on traditional Thai spring rolls, using Uruguayan empanada dough and adding a sweet and sour mirin glaze.
instructions: Preheat oil in a deep frying pan to 350°F (180°C).
Roll out the empanada dough thinly and cut into desired shapes.
Heat a tablespoon of oil in a pan over medium heat. Add spring onion and bitter melon; cook until softened.
Add cilantro, soy sauce, and sugar to the pan; stir-fry for 1 minute.
Brush the empanada shapes with water and place on a plate.
Place a tablespoon of the spring onion mixture onto the center of each empanada.
Fold the empanada in half to enclose the filling and press the edges together to seal.
Fry the empanada in hot oil until golden brown, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Drain excess oil on paper towels.
Drizzle with mirin glaze and serve immediately.
